title: Marbella Dolphin Watching Cheap Boat Hire: 2026 Prices & Tips
html:
Marbella offers dolphin-watching trips from Puerto Deportivo de Marbella for as little as EUR 45 per person when you share a guided tour. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators so you can book a charter online without overpaying. The Mediterranean here hosts common and striped dolphins almost year-round.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most dolphin-watching departures use Puerto Deportivo de Marbella or Puerto Banús. From Puerto Deportivo, boats reach the Cabopino area in 20 minutes, where dolphins often feed near the Cabo Pino lighthouse. Puerto Banús is the larger marina with dozens of charter operators and direct access to the Bay of Marbella. Other launch points include the small fishing port of La Bajadilla, the sandy beach at El Faro, and the coastal town of Estepona, 20 kilometres south, where local skippers run cheaper shared trips. The waters off the Marbella coastline are part of the Alboran Sea, a recognised marine corridor for cetaceans.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching

  1. Shared rigid inflatable boat (RIB), best for speed and proximity to dolphins, up to 12 guests, from EUR 55 per person for 2 hours.
  2. Small motorboat (Azimut 36), comfortable for a private group of 6, 8, half-day from EUR 450, skippered only.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon 46), stable and family-friendly, up to 12 guests, full-day charter from EUR 1,200 including crew.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (Mefasa 90), luxury option for up to 20 guests, daily rate from EUR 2,900, ideal for groups wanting a guaranteed sighting; see this motor yacht on BoatHire24 for full specs.

How much does a dolphin-watching trip cost in Marbella?

  • Shared tour on a RIB: EUR 45, 70 per adult for 2 hours, children half price.
  • Private motorboat half-day: EUR 400, 600 depending on size and season.
  • Catamaran full-day hire: EUR 900, 1,500 with crew and snacks.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (10+ guests): EUR 2,500, 4,000 per day, often including lunch and open bar.

All charter prices exclude fuel unless stated. You can compare prices across operators and book a charter online with transparent listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

No licence is required for skippered charters, which cover almost all dolphin-watching trips. If you plan to rent a bareboat motorboat under 6 metres, Spanish law demands a valid recreational licence; the operator confirms exact requirements at time of booking. High season in Marbella runs June through September, when the local Poniente wind blows from the west and can create light chop in the afternoon. For the calmest dolphin sightings, book a morning trip.

Book a morning departure from Puerto Deportivo in July or August to avoid the afternoon Poniente wind, which can push small boats into choppy water near Cabopino.

At a glance

  • Price per person: EUR 45, 70
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours (shared) to 8 hours (private)
  • Max group size: 8, 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I see dolphins in Marbella every day?

Dolphins are resident in the Alboran Sea, so sightings are common on most trips, but no operator can guarantee them because marine life is wild.

What is the best time of year for dolphin watching in Marbella?

May to October offers the warmest sea temperatures and highest sighting rates, with June and July being particularly reliable for common dolphins.

Are children allowed on dolphin-watching charters?

Yes, most shared RIB trips and all private charters welcome children, often at reduced rates, provided life jackets are worn.

How far offshore do the dolphins appear?

Dolphins are typically spotted 2 to 5 nautical miles from the Marbella coast, often near the Cabopino lighthouse or across the Bay of Marbella.

Whether you are visiting Marbella for a day or a month, renting a boat for dolphin watching is a straightforward way to see the Alboran Sea's wildlife.
